pattern: Since same-parity digits can be freely permuted amongst themselves, sorting the even digits and the odd digits independently (each in descending order) and dropping them back into their original positions rebuilds the largest reachable number in one pass, no simulated swapping needed.
hints: Swaps are only allowed between two digits of the same parity, but any number of swaps is allowed -- that means every even digit can end up in any even digit's original slot, and likewise for odd digits.
Split the digits into two groups by parity while remembering which positions were even and which were odd, then sort each group in descending order on its own.

Walk the original digit string left to right: whenever you see a position that held an even digit, place the next unused even digit (largest remaining first) there; do the same for odd positions with the sorted odd digits.

edge_inputs.description: single digit number, nothing to swap
all digits share one parity, already maximal after sorting
all digits are the same value, swaps change nothing
leading digit is the smallest odd digit while a larger odd digit sits later
contains a zero digit that must stay grouped with the evens

stress.description: random positive integers spanning short to the full constraint length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    digits = rng.randint(1, 10)
    lo = 10 ** (digits - 1) if digits > 1 else 1
    hi = (10 ** digits) - 1
    hi = min(hi, 1000000000)
    num = rng.randint(lo, hi)
    return [num]
